description: KLOEL multi-agent orchestration manifest — defines available agent types, their capabilities, and coordination contracts for Hermes, OpenHands, and Claude Code CLI

claude-code.label: Claude Code CLI
claude-code.description: Anthropic Claude Code CLI — primary implementation agent. Handles all Risk 1-2 tasks: frontend, hooks, API clients, backend services. Reads CLAUDE.md, AGENTS.md, and CODEX.md on boot. Follows Agent Operating Protocol.

hermes.label: Agente Hermes
hermes.description: Agente Hermes — specialized review and quality agent. Conducts code reviews, security audits, architecture validation, and quality gate enforcement. Runs Codacy integrity checks, ratchet measurements, and governance boundary validation. Read-only by default; can suggest fixes but must not directly edit protected files.

modes.review: Post-implementation code review against AGENT_RUNBOOK.md standards
modes.audit: Security, architecture, and governance boundary audit
modes.quality: Codacy max-rigor enforcement, ratchet check, dead-code analysis

openhands.label: OpenHands
openhands.description: OpenHands — autonomous development agent with E2E planning through deployment capability. Handles multi-step feature implementation, infrastructure changes (Railway), and full-stack tasks. Scoped per worktree for isolation. Follows the same AGENT_RUNBOOK.md protocol with PULSE integration.

planner.label: PULSE Planner Agent
planner.description: PULSE autonomous planner — uses @openai/agents SDK to decompose work, rank units by readiness, and dispatch parallel agents. Runs in deterministic mode when Agents SDK is unavailable. Controls the autonomous loop: inspect → plan → dispatch → verify → commit.

coordinationContract.conflictResolution: orchestrator applies patches serially, regression guard on each merge
